title: PM Vishwakarma Yojana — Free Training, Tools & Loans for Artisans
meta_description: PM Vishwakarma 2026 — free training + ₹15K toolkit grant + ₹3L loan for 18 traditional trades. Eligibility, how to apply, benefits guide.

hero.one_liner: Free skill training, modern toolkits, and collateral-free loans up to ₹3 lakh for traditional artisans and craftspeople across 18 trades

what_is_it: PM Vishwakarma Yojana is a central government scheme launched on 17 September 2023 (Vishwakarma Jayanti) with a total outlay of ₹13,000 crore over 5 years (2023-2028). It provides end-to-end support to traditional artisans and craftspeople working with their hands and tools — including carpenters, blacksmiths, goldsmiths, potters, weavers, sculptors, cobblers, tailors, and 10 other identified trades.

The scheme has three core components: (1) Skill training — free 5-7 day basic training + 15 day advanced training with a daily stipend of ₹500, (2) Toolkit incentive — up to ₹15,000 grant (not loan) to buy modern tools, and (3) Credit support — collateral-free loan of up to ₹1 lakh (first tranche) and up to ₹2 lakh additional (second tranche) at a subsidized interest rate of 5% (government provides 8% interest subvention on loans).

The 18 eligible trades are: Carpenter, Boat Maker, Armourer, Blacksmith, Hammer and Tool Kit Maker, Locksmith, Goldsmith, Potter, Sculptor/Stone Carver, Cobbler/Shoemaker, Mason/Bricklayer, Basket/Mat/Broom Maker, Doll & Toy Maker (traditional), Barber, Garland Maker, Washerman, Tailor, and Fishing Net Maker. The scheme recognizes these time-honored professions and modernizes them.

To be eligible, the artisan must be working in one of these 18 trades with their hands and tools as a self-employed profession. The person should be at least 18 years old and should not have availed similar benefits under other central/state schemes in the last 5 years. Registration is done through the PM Vishwakarma portal with verification by local authorities.

eligibility.label: Who can apply
eligibility.value: Self-employed artisans/craftspeople working in any of the 18 identified trades with their hands and tools

eligibility.label: Age
eligibility.value: Minimum 18 years at the time of registration

eligibility.label: Family limit
eligibility.value: One member per family (family = husband, wife, and unmarried children)

eligibility.label: Not eligible if
eligibility.value: Government employees, people who availed similar scheme benefits in last 5 years, or those registered under PM Mudra/PMEGP currently

eligibility.label: Verification
eligibility.value: Application verified by Gram Panchayat (rural) or Urban Local Body (urban) at the ward level

eligibility.label: Registration
eligibility.value: Online at pmvishwakarma.gov.in — needs Aadhaar, mobile number, bank account, and trade-related self-declaration

extra_sections.heading: 18 Eligible Trades — Complete List
rows: Carpenter, Blacksmith, Locksmith, Mason/Bricklayer, Armourer, Hammer & Tool Kit Maker
Boat Maker, Basket/Mat/Broom Maker, Doll & Toy Maker (traditional), Barber, Garland Maker, Fishing Net Maker

extra_sections.heading: 5-Stage Benefits Package
content:
Stage 1 — Official Recognition

After registration and verification by local authorities, you receive a PM Vishwakarma certificate and ID card. This official recognition gives you credibility in the market and access to all scheme benefits.

Stage 2 — Free Skill Training (₹500/day stipend)

Attend 5-7 days of basic training + 15 days of advanced training at a government-identified training center. Receive ₹500/day as stipend during training (total ₹10,000-11,000).
Training covers modern techniques, quality improvement, design upgradation, and digital marketing skills relevant to your trade.

Stage 3 — Toolkit Incentive (₹15,000 grant)

After completing training, receive up to ₹15,000 as a one-time grant (NOT a loan — you don't repay it). This amount is credited directly to your bank account.
Use it to buy modern tools: a carpenter can buy power drill + saw, tailor can buy advanced sewing machine, barber can buy electric clippers, etc.

Stage 4 — First Loan Tranche (₹1 lakh at 5%)

Apply for a collateral-free loan of up to ₹1 lakh at an effective interest rate of 5%. Loan tenure is 18 months.
No security deposit needed. Repayment is through EMIs.

Stage 5 — Second Loan Tranche (₹2 lakh at 5%)

After successfully repaying the first loan, apply for an additional loan of up to ₹2 lakh at the same subsidized 5% rate for business expansion. Total credit available = ₹3 lakh.

extra_sections.heading: Additional Support Beyond Basic Benefits
content:
Digital Payment Incentive

Receive ₹1 per UPI transaction (up to 100 transactions/month = ₹100/month) for going digital. This encourages cashless transactions and helps you build digital payment history.

Market Linkage & E-Commerce Onboarding

Get support to sell your products on: GeM (Government e-Marketplace), Amazon Saheli, Flipkart Samarth, and other approved e-commerce platforms. Helps artisans reach national and international markets.

Quality Certification

Receive Bharat Mark or other quality certifications that increase product value and help you command premium prices in the market.

Branding Support

Assistance with product branding, packaging design, and labeling to make your products more attractive to customers.

steps.title: Register at pmvishwakarma.gov.in
steps.description: Visit pmvishwakarma.gov.in, click 'Register', enter Aadhaar number, verify with OTP. Fill personal details, trade details, upload a photo. You can also register through CSC centers.

steps.title: Local verification by Gram Panchayat/ULB
steps.description: Your application is sent to the local Gram Panchayat (rural) or Urban Local Body (urban). They verify your trade and self-employment status through a physical visit or video call. Verification takes 7-15 days.

steps.title: Receive PM Vishwakarma certificate
steps.description: After verification, you receive a digital certificate and ID card via email. This is your official badge as a Vishwakarma artisan — eligible for all scheme benefits.

steps.title: Attend skill training (5-7 days + 15 days)
steps.description: Enroll for training at the nearest government-identified training center. Attend basic training (5-7 days, ₹500/day stipend) + advanced training (15 days, ₹500/day stipend). Total stipend = ₹10,000-11,000.

steps.title: Receive toolkit grant (₹15,000)
steps.description: After training completion, ₹15,000 is credited directly to your bank account. Use it to purchase modern tools relevant to your trade.

steps.title: Apply for loans (₹1L + ₹2L)
steps.description: After toolkit disbursement, apply for the ₹1 lakh first loan through pmvishwakarma.gov.in. Loan is processed within 15-20 days. After repaying first loan, apply for ₹2 lakh second loan.

how_to_apply.title: Register at pmvishwakarma.gov.in
how_to_apply.description: Visit the portal, enter Aadhaar, verify details. Fill trade and personal information.

how_to_apply.title: Local verification by Panchayat/ULB
how_to_apply.description: Takes 7-15 days. Confirm your trade and self-employment status.

how_to_apply.title: Receive PM Vishwakarma certificate
how_to_apply.description: Digital certificate + ID card. Eligible for all benefits.

how_to_apply.title: Claim toolkit grant (₹15,000)
how_to_apply.description: Credited to bank after training completion.

important_alert.text: Registration is FREE. Do not pay any money to middlemen or agents claiming to expedite the process. All registration is through the official portal only. Only one family member can register. If you face issues, call helpline 1800-267-7777 or visit your nearest CSC center.

important_dates.label: Registration
important_dates.value: Open throughout the year at pmvishwakarma.gov.in

faqs.question: What are the 18 eligible trades?
faqs.answer: Carpenter, Boat Maker, Armourer, Blacksmith, Hammer & Tool Kit Maker, Locksmith, Goldsmith, Potter, Sculptor/Stone Carver, Cobbler/Shoemaker, Mason/Bricklayer, Basket/Mat/Broom Maker, Doll & Toy Maker, Barber, Garland Maker, Washerman, Tailor, and Fishing Net Maker. You must be self-employed in one of these trades.

faqs.question: Can a tailor or barber from a salon apply?
faqs.answer: Only if you're self-employed. If you work as an employee in someone else's salon or shop, you're not eligible. But if you run your own tailoring shop or barber shop (sole proprietor), you can definitely apply for PM Vishwakarma.

faqs.question: I already have a PM Mudra loan. Can I apply?
faqs.answer: If you currently have an active PM Mudra or PMEGP loan, you may not be eligible for PM Vishwakarma credit. However, you can still register for the recognition, training, and toolkit components (₹15,000 grant). Check eligibility details on the portal or call the helpline.

faqs.question: Is the ₹15,000 toolkit amount a loan or grant?
faqs.answer: It's a GRANT — you don't need to repay it. The ₹15,000 is credited directly to your bank account after completing the skill training. Only the credit support (₹1-3 lakh) is a loan that needs to be repaid with interest.

faqs.question: How long does local verification take?
faqs.answer: Gram Panchayat/ULB verification typically takes 7-15 days after registration. If delayed, follow up at your local Panchayat office with your application number or contact the helpline. Some states have expedited the process with digital verification.

faqs.question: What if my trade is not in the 18 listed?
faqs.answer: Unfortunately, only the 18 specified trades are eligible for PM Vishwakarma. If your trade is not listed, check if you can classify it under one of the 18 (for example, furniture maker might fall under 'Carpenter'). If not, you may be eligible for other artisan schemes like PM Mudra Yojana or state-level craft schemes.

faqs.question: Can I apply for both first and second loan together?
faqs.answer: No. You must first apply for and receive the ₹1 lakh first loan, repay it successfully (18 months), and then apply for the ₹2 lakh second loan. This staged approach ensures you develop loan repayment discipline before accessing larger credit.
